source_pr.title: test: optimize dll test
source_pr.body:
### What problem does this PR solve?
make ddl unit test running faster
### What is changed and how it works?
One loop in the hot path cost  almost 10s to execute, and several test cases depends on this function. The loop run 100 times to randomly select a range of 3 to check whether the result is right, this is unneccssary. We can reduce it to 20 times with check the lower/upper boundary and some random inner case is enough to make sure the logic is right.

Here is the result:
Before change:
```
>  go test -v -vet=off -p 10 -race github.com/pingcap/tidb/ddl
ok  	ok  	github.com/pingcap/tidb/ddl	200.443s
```

After change:
```
> go test -v -vet=off -p 10 -race github.com/pingcap/tidb/ddl
ok  	github.com/pingcap/tidb/ddl	118.154s
```

### Check List
Tests

- unit tests

Code changes

Side effects

Related changes

symptoms: A DDL test uses `rand.Intn(...)` to pick index-range cases, making the covered inputs nondeterministic and sometimes hitting problematic boundaries.
failure_signature: Flaky assertion in `ddl/db_test.go` driven by randomized index selection (`rand.Intn`), where some random choices can produce unexpected range results.
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it relied on randomized test inputs: using `rand.Intn(...)` to pick index-range cases can lead to nondeterministic coverage and sporadic failures when certain boundary indices are selected. The PR stabilizes it by replacing the random selection with deterministic boundary/step-based cases, making the test input predictable.
fix_pattern: Replace randomized test input (`rand.Intn`, time-based seeds, probabilistic picks) with deterministic, boundary-focused cases or a fixed seed so assertions are stable.
